name: Justice Finance
description_short: Justice Finance is a privately owned DFW-area consumer lender offering signature installment loans up to $1,800 across 18 brick-and-mortar locations. No checking account required.
description_long:
Justice Finance is a privately owned consumer finance company operating exclusively in the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex. With 18 branch locations spread throughout the DFW area, the company has built a local footprint serving residents who need small-dollar installment financing. The firm explicitly distinguishes itself from payday lenders and online-only platforms, positioning itself as a community-based lender where in-person relationships are central to the experience.

The company's core product is a cash installment loan of up to $1,800. All loans are signature-only — no collateral, vehicle title, or security deposit is required. Borrowers can apply online, by visiting a branch, or by calling a location directly. After submission, loan officers validate the application and follow up for any clarification before issuing a decision. Upon approval (subject to credit requirements), borrowers come in to collect their funds. Repayment is structured as scheduled installment payments; one reviewer noted paying approximately $100 per month on a $1,000 loan. Notably, the company does not require a checking account, which broadens access for unbanked and underbanked consumers.

What distinguishes Justice Finance from national online lenders is its emphasis on neighborhood branches and face-to-face service. Customer reviews consistently highlight professional, friendly staff across multiple locations, with several reviewers citing the same staff members by name across years of repeat business. The company also works with applicants who have poor or thin credit histories — multiple reviews specifically reference approval despite bad credit — suggesting more flexible underwriting than prime lenders.

Justice Finance fills a real gap for DFW-area borrowers with limited credit options who need a small installment loan and prefer in-person service over an anonymous online process. However, the website discloses no APR ranges, no example total loan costs, and no information on typical repayment term lengths — a significant transparency gap prospective borrowers should resolve by calling a branch directly before applying. The $1,800 maximum makes this unsuitable for anyone needing more than a modest bridge loan, and the firm has no presence outside the DFW metroplex, making it irrelevant to out-of-state consumers.

As a financial institution, this lender competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders in the consumer lending space. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans. Many of these lenders offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
address: 2420 A Jacksboro Hwy, Fort Worth, TX 76114

company_info.headquarters: 2420 A Jacksboro Hwy, Fort Worth, TX 76114

pros: No checking account required — accessible to unbanked borrowers
Signature-only loans — no collateral or vehicle title needed
18 DFW branch locations for in-person service
Works with bad credit applicants — multiple reviews confirm approvals despite poor credit history
Installment payment structure rather than lump-sum repayment
Multiple application channels: online, in-person, or by phone
Consistent staff recognition in reviews suggests stable, trained personnel
cons: No APR, rate range, or example loan cost disclosed on the website — zero pricing transparency
Strictly DFW-area only — no service outside the metroplex
Maximum loan of $1,800 is low — unsuitable for any meaningful financial need beyond a small shortfall
Funds require an in-person branch visit to collect — not a digital disbursement option
No information on loan terms, minimum credit score, or income requirements published online
best_for: DFW residents with bad or thin credit who need a small installment loan
Unbanked or underbanked borrowers who do not have a checking account
Consumers who prefer face-to-face service over online-only lenders
Repeat borrowers seeking a local lender they can build a relationship with
diagnosis: Justice Finance is best suited for DFW-area borrowers with poor credit and no bank account who need a small installment loan under $1,800 and value in-person service. The main caveat is complete lack of APR or cost disclosure on the website — consumers should get a full rate quote and repayment schedule in writing before signing anything.
services: In-person branch applications at 18 DFW locations
Loan application validation and review by branch staff
